name: Number Series

sourceRefs.label: tnpscguru.in - 8 real past-paper number series questions, independently re-verified

researchNotes: This is the LAST of Part B's 6 named Reasoning skills, completing all of Part B (Aptitude & Mental Ability) entirely. 8 sourced questions, every single one independently re-derived and verified by hand before use (one item's source-site number formatting was garbled — likely 'prime² ' exponents flattened into plain digits — caught and corrected to present the actual mathematical pattern rather than propagate the confusing raw text).

reading.intro: Number Series is the final Reasoning skill. Real exam evidence shows 6 distinct underlying patterns: second-difference (difference-of-differences) sequences, sum-of-consecutive-cubes, geometric progression, arithmetic progression, n² multiples, and prime-number-squared sequences — recognize the pattern TYPE before trying to compute term-by-term.

qa.question: Method — sum of consecutive cubes: Find the next term: 9, 35, 91, ?
Method — second-difference (arithmetic sequence of differences): Fill in the blank: 1, 6, 15, 28, 45, ___, 91.
qa.answer: Differences: 6−1=5, 15−6=9, 28−15=13, 45−28=17 — these differences themselves increase by 4 each time. Next difference = 17+4=21, so the blank = 45+21 = 66. (Check: 91−66=25=21+4 ✓ consistent.)

qa.question: Method — sequence of squared primes: Find the missing number in the sequence of squared prime numbers: 4 (2²), 9 (3²), 25 (5²), 49 (7²), ?, 169 (13²).
qa.answer: The base numbers are consecutive primes: 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13. The missing term corresponds to 11² = 121. (Caution: the original source rendered this sequence with garbled formatting that looked like plain 3-digit numbers rather than squared primes — always re-derive the actual pattern rather than trusting a confusing transcription.)

qa.question: Method — simple arithmetic progression (AP) with increasing-by-2 differences: Find the next term: 1, 5, 11, 19, 29, ?
qa.answer: Differences: 4, 6, 8, 10 — increasing by 2 each time. Next difference = 12, so next term = 29+12 = 41.

qa.question: Method — geometric progression (GP): Find the 8th term of 2, 6, 18, 54, ...
qa.answer: Common ratio = 3 (each term ×3). Terms: 2,6,18,54,162,486,1458,4374. The 8th term is 4,374.

qa.question: Method — plain arithmetic progression, nth term formula: Find the 20th term of 7, 10, 13, ...
qa.answer: First term=7, common difference=3. nth term = a+(n−1)d = 7+(19×3) = 7+57 = 64.

qa.question: Method — multiples of n²: Find the missing number: 3, 12, 27, 48, 75, 108, ___.

questions.explanation: Differences: 5,7,9,11 — increasing by 2. Next difference=13, so blank=34+13=47. (Check: 62−47=15=13+2, consistent.)
Differences: 4,8,12,16 — increasing by 4. Next difference=20, so next term=43+20=63.

questions.prompt: Find the 7th term of the GP: 3, 9, 27, 81, ...
Find the 15th term of the AP: 5, 9, 13, 17, ...
